prompt:
The claims coordinator is reconciling two claims for the review queue. Produce `audit_brief.md` from full claim records, not search summaries. Find the claim named “Warehouse Theft Claim” at “West Office” and the claim named “Fleet Windshield Claim” at “North Office”.

Use the genuine `./claimdesk` executable in the workspace. Before any claim-data action, invoke its top-level live help with `./claimdesk --help`. Then issue both name-and-location searches together in one assistant action. Only after that action returns may you retrieve a branch, and only when its search result contains exactly one stable ID. If both branches resolve, issue both record retrievals together in the next assistant action. A missing or ambiguous branch stays unresolved without guessing, while an independently resolved branch should still be completed. The executable records the live-help inspection and staged actions for the protected acceptance check.

This is a read-only audit. Do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not infer a status from a search summary; use the retrieved full record.

The brief must contain exactly two Markdown bullet lines, in the requested claim order, with no heading or other text. Use these exact shapes (replacing angle-bracket placeholders):
- `- Warehouse Theft Claim (West Office): <status-or-UNRESOLVED>`
- `- Fleet Windshield Claim (North Office): <status-or-UNRESOLVED>; comparison: <same|different|unavailable>`

Use `same` or `different` only when both statuses were retrieved; otherwise use `unavailable`. Run `python3 protected_tests/verify.py` when finished. The acceptance files are protected.
